title: Transformer Causal Self-Attention
constraints: Apply a lower-triangular causal mask before softmax.
Query position i may attend only to key positions j <= i.
Future positions must receive attention probability 0.
Return the final attention output, not the weights.
statement:
Write a Python function `transformer_causal_self_attention(Q, K, V)` that computes causal scaled dot-product self-attention.

Causal attention prevents a token from seeing future tokens. First compute scores

$$
S = \frac{QK^T}{\sqrt{d_k}}.
$$

Then apply the causal mask

$$
M_{ij} = 1 \text{ if } j \le i, \qquad M_{ij} = 0 \text{ if } j > i.
$$

After masked row-wise softmax, return the value mixture

$$
O = \operatorname{softmax}(S_{masked})V.
$$

## Input / Output
- `Q`: `numpy.ndarray` of shape `(seq_len, d_k)`.
- `K`: `numpy.ndarray` of shape `(seq_len, d_k)`.
- `V`: `numpy.ndarray` of shape `(seq_len, d_v)`.
- Returns: `numpy.ndarray` of shape `(seq_len, d_v)` containing causal self-attention outputs.
reference_solution:
import numpy as np

def transformer_causal_self_attention(Q, K, V):
    Q = np.asarray(Q, dtype=float)
    K = np.asarray(K, dtype=float)
    V = np.asarray(V, dtype=float)
    scores = (Q @ K.T) / np.sqrt(K.shape[1])
    seq_len = scores.shape[0]
    mask = np.tril(np.ones((seq_len, seq_len)))
    masked_scores = np.where(mask == 1, scores, -np.inf)
    shifted = masked_scores - np.max(masked_scores, axis=1, keepdims=True)
    exp_scores = np.exp(shifted)
    exp_scores = np.where(mask == 1, exp_scores, 0.0)
    weights = exp_scores / np.sum(exp_scores, axis=1, keepdims=True)
    return weights @ V
solution_explanation: The function combines scaled dot-product attention with a lower-triangular mask. The first token can only use its own value, while later tokens can mix previous values and their own value.
